behavior_summary: This script implements the 'U8P_LONGWEB::gotHit' event for a LONGWEB instance: when the web is struck, it records hit-related state on the object (via a set-info / process-exclude sequence) and then immediately destroys the web instance.
The routine does not inspect or branch on the numeric parameters; it performs a fixed sequence of state updates and cleanup, making the web a one-shot destructible object on hit.
notable_control_flow: Single linear path: mark the object (set info / process exclude) then call Item::destroy on the instance.
No conditional branches or global-flag checks observed; parameters supplied to the event are not used to affect control flow.
Effectively a one-time trigger: after recording the hit state the object is removed from the world.
open_questions: What exact info ID and field(s) are set by the 'set info' step (the disassembly shows a constant push but the semantic meaning of that info key is not named)?
Which object receives the 'set info' (the instructions derive a target from the item but the exact target relationship is inferred rather than explicit)?
What do the two declared parameters (uword, word) represent in gameplay terms (attacker PID, hit source ID, damage amount, etc.) — they are not used in this handler, so their role is uncertain).

main_function.name: U8P_LONGWEB::gotHit
main_function.purpose: Handle a hit event on a LONGWEB object by marking it as hit/excluding it from further processing and then destroying the item so the web is removed from the game world.
entry_conditions: Event 6 (U8P_LONGWEB::gotHit) — invoked when the LONGWEB object is struck or receives damage

function_map.name: U8P_LONGWEB::gotHit
function_map.role: Event handler for 'U8P_LONGWEB::gotHit': mark hit state / process-exclude and destroy the LONGWEB instance.

external_calls.purpose: Record a hit-related info/state on a target object and exclude that object from further processing (prevents further handling before destruction). This is an inferred engine operation rather than a normal function call.
Remove and clean up the LONGWEB item instance from the game world (destructible cleanup). Called with the item instance pointer supplied to the event.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the 'U8P_LONGWEB::gotHit' event for a LONGWEB instance: when the web is struck, it records hit-related state on the object (via a set-info / process-exclude sequence) and then immediately destroys the web instance.
